definition: Unique row identifier for the merged ClinicalTrials.gov effect row.
notes: Author rows keep the author effect identifier as the row identifier.

definition: Identifier for the author-reported outcome-analysis effect row.
notes: Used to trace rows back to source-reported analyses.

definition: Identifier for the selected raw endpoint calculation row.
notes: Used to trace rows back to raw result-group calculations.

definition: Source of the effect estimate used for b, se, z, and z_operator.
notes: Use include_in_bear, not effect_source alone, for the main BEAR inclusion rule.

definition: Whether this row is used by the main BEAR ClinicalTrials.gov build. TRUE keeps author-reported rows and raw-derived rows only when no author-reported overlap is available.
notes: Use this field to follow the rule: use author-reported effects when available, otherwise use raw-derived effects.

definition: Import decision for the row after author-reported and raw-derived overlaps are compared.
notes: Rows marked exclude_author_preferred_overlap are retained for linkage/audit but not used by the main BEAR build.

definition: Shared key for linking author-reported rows to raw-derived rows for the same study, outcome, and result-group pair.
allowed_values_or_units: nct_id, outcome_id, and result-group pair key
missingness: missing when no group pair is available
notes: Joining by this key can return multiple raw rows for one author row because ClinicalTrials.gov can store many measurement strata under the same outcome and groups.

definition: ClinicalTrials.gov study identifier.
notes: Primary study-level identifier.

definition: AACT outcome identifier linked to the effect row.
notes: Identifies the registry outcome.

definition: AACT outcome-analysis identifier for source-reported analyses.
notes: Raw-only rows are derived from result-group measurements rather than outcome-analysis rows.

definition: Brief trial title.
Official trial title.
ClinicalTrials.gov study type.
notes: ClinicalTrials.gov contains other study types; the main BEAR ClinicalTrials.gov dataset keeps interventional randomized trial rows.

definition: Trial phase as recorded by ClinicalTrials.gov.
ClinicalTrials.gov recruitment or completion status.
notes: The public analytic file keeps completed studies only.

definition: Year used by BEAR for time summaries.
notes: Taken from completion timing when available and otherwise from posted-results timing.

definition: Trial enrollment count.
Whether enrollment is actual or anticipated.
Number of intervention arms recorded in the ClinicalTrials.gov study design table.
Number of observational groups recorded in the ClinicalTrials.gov study design table; usually missing for interventional trials.
Calendar year when results were first posted.
notes: Derived from results_first_posted_date.

definition: Calendar year of primary completion.
notes: Derived from primary_completion_date.

definition: Number of public candidate effect rows linked to the same ClinicalTrials.gov study.
notes: Counts include author-reported rows, raw-only rows, and overlapping raw rows retained for linkage/audit. The main BEAR build excludes studies with 20 or more candidate rows.

definition: Number of distinct outcome IDs represented for the same ClinicalTrials.gov study in the public file.
notes: Primary outcome can be represented by more than one ClinicalTrials.gov outcome ID.

definition: Allocation design recorded for the study. The main BEAR ClinicalTrials.gov dataset keeps randomized rows; non-randomized and missing-allocation rows remain identifiable in the public file.
notes: ClinicalTrials.gov includes non-randomized and missing-allocation records; the main BEAR mixture analysis filters to randomized interventional trials.

definition: Intervention model recorded for the study. The main BEAR ClinicalTrials.gov dataset does not filter on intervention model, although the raw-derived endpoint path is restricted to eligible randomized parallel interventional trials.
Primary purpose recorded for the study. The main BEAR ClinicalTrials.gov dataset does not filter on primary purpose.
Observational model if applicable; this is usually present only for observational studies and is therefore missing for most interventional rows.
notes: Most retained rows are interventional studies.

definition: Masking design recorded for the study.
Whether the intervention model indicates a factorial design.
Whether the intervention model indicates a crossover design.
Number of design-group records linked to the study. Unlike number_of_arms, this is counted from the design_groups table and is available for comparator summaries.
notes: Study-level arm-count summary.

definition: Whether any design-group record appears to be a placebo comparator.
notes: Based on design-group text.

definition: Compact study-level comparator summary derived from design-group labels and descriptions.
Whether the study has more than two arms or groups.
Whether baseline measurement rows were available in AACT.
All mapped clinical domains for the trial.
notes: Mapped from AACT conditions and MeSH terms.

definition: Number of mapped clinical domains for the trial.
Source used for domain mapping.
Whether the trial maps to oncology.
Primary mapped clinical domain.
All intervention types recorded for the study.
Number of intervention types recorded for the study.
Primary intervention type used for summaries.
Whether any intervention is coded as drug.
Whether any intervention is coded as biological.
Whether any intervention is coded as device.
Whether any intervention is coded as procedure.
Whether any intervention is coded as behavioral.
Whether intervention text indicates a vaccine trial.
Whether intervention text indicates cell or gene therapy.
Whether any intervention is coded as diagnostic test.
Lead sponsor name.
Lead sponsor class.
Whether the lead sponsor class is industry.
Outcome type recorded in ClinicalTrials.gov.
notes: ClinicalTrials.gov contains secondary and other outcome types; this analytic file keeps primary outcomes.

definition: Outcome title.
Outcome time frame.
AACT measurement stratum identifier for raw-derived rows.
Measurement title for raw-derived endpoint rows.
Measurement units for raw-derived endpoint rows.
Effect-size family used by BEAR summaries.
Scale of the stored effect size.
Effect estimate used for BEAR-facing analysis.
notes: Alias of b retained for compatibility.

definition: Effect estimate used for z-value calculations where available.
Standard error of the effect estimate where available.
Signed z-value used in BEAR analyses.
notes: Rows without a recoverable z-value are not included in this analytic file.

definition: Operator describing whether z is exact or bounded.
notes: For p-value inequalities the direction is converted to the z scale.

definition: Number of p-value sides assumed for the z-value derivation.
notes: ClinicalTrials.gov p-values are treated as two-sided in this workflow.

definition: Result-group identifiers linked to an author-reported outcome analysis.
Group codes linked to an author-reported outcome analysis.
Number of result groups linked to an author-reported analysis.
Canonical result-group pair key for author-reported rows.
missingness: missing when no pair can be formed
notes: Used only as public provenance for the source-reported comparison.

definition: Statistical method reported for the outcome analysis.
Parameter type reported for the outcome analysis.
Source-reported effect estimate.
missingness: missing for raw-only rows and some p-only analyses

definition: Source-reported confidence interval lower bound.
missingness: missing when no interval is reported

definition: Source-reported confidence interval upper bound.
missingness: missing when no interval is reported

definition: Source-reported p-value.
missingness: missing when no p-value is reported

definition: Source-reported p-value inequality modifier.
missingness: missing for exact or absent p-values

definition: Confidence level of the source-reported interval.
missingness: missing when no interval is reported

definition: Number of sides for the source-reported confidence interval.
missingness: missing when no interval is reported

definition: Raw endpoint effect measure selected for BEAR import.
notes: Only selected raw endpoint scales enter the public file; non-selected raw scales remain in audit artifacts.

definition: Raw endpoint outcome family.
Number of selected raw endpoint rows matching the same study, outcome, and result-group pair for an author-reported row.
notes: Values greater than 1 indicate multiple raw measurement strata for the same author comparison; join with care.

definition: Rule used to derive the raw endpoint contrast.
Event count in the treatment or focal group for binary raw contrasts.
missingness: missing for continuous rows and author-reported rows

definition: Event count in the comparator group for binary raw contrasts.
missingness: missing for continuous rows and author-reported rows

definition: Analysis count in the treatment or focal group for raw contrasts.
Analysis count in the comparator group for raw contrasts.
Mean in the treatment or focal group for continuous raw contrasts.
missingness: missing for binary rows and author-reported rows

definition: Mean in the comparator group for continuous raw contrasts.
missingness: missing for binary rows and author-reported rows

definition: Standard deviation in the treatment or focal group for continuous raw contrasts.
missingness: missing for binary rows and author-reported rows

definition: Standard deviation in the comparator group for continuous raw contrasts.
missingness: missing for binary rows and author-reported rows

definition: Canonical result-group pair key for raw-derived contrasts.
AACT result-group identifier for the treatment or focal group.
AACT result-group identifier for the comparator group.
Title of the treatment or focal result group.
Title of the comparator result group.
Role assigned to the treatment or focal group.
Role assigned to the comparator group.
Whether treatment-comparator direction could not be inferred for the raw-derived contrast.
notes: TRUE means the sign of the raw-derived contrast is less interpretable.

definition: Confidence level of the raw group-role assignment.
Dependency class for the raw result-group pair within the study outcome.
Whether the raw-derived contrast comes from a multi-arm trial.
